candidate_problems.text: Which specific feedback loops most reliably distinguish cities that become safer over decades from superficially comparable cities that do not: institutional legitimacy, youth opportunity, public-space maintenance, governance fragmentation, or dispute-resolution capacity?
How can one test whether a city's apparent crime decline came from redesigning underlying coordination systems rather than from narrower enforcement tactics or demographic shifts?

What inherited institutional structures—patronage, fiscal instability, fragmented governance, racial exclusion—most strongly condition whether the same safety intervention succeeds or fails across cities?
